content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 19, 1994


Marie Milne
Office of Planning
and Research
1400 Tenth Street, Room 150
Sacramento, Ca 95814 A-8

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-260

Dear Ms. Milne:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding
whether the Governor's Task Force On Information Policy and
Technology Procurement (GTFIPTP) is an "agency" subject to
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act.")1/

QUESTIONS

1. Must GTFIPTP adopt a conflict of interest code?

2. Must members of GTFIPTP and its staff file statements of
economic interests?

CONCLUSIONS

1. GTFIPTP has no obligation to adopt a conflict of
interest code.

2. Neither members of the GTFIPTP nor its staff have any
obligation to file statements of economic interests by virtue of
their positions with GTFIPTP.

FACTS

GTFIPTP was established on May 11, 1994, by Executive Order
W-88-94 to modernize the state's technologies and procurement
policies.

GTFIPTP will conduct a review of the state's information and
technology procurement practices, and the manner in which the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5600

Milne, Marie
No. A-94-260
Page 2


state plans for, manages and oversees the development of
information systems.

The Governor has appointed seven individuals to GTFIPTP who
have experience in technology and information systems management
and acquisition in large private and nonprofit organizations.

The Governor's Office of Planning and Research will provide
support to GTFIPTP and will be responsible for administration of
contracts.

GTFIPTP will report its progress to the Governor no later
than August 15, 1994.

ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 87300 requires every agency to adopt
and promulgate a conflict of interest code. A conflict of
interest code is a document which specifically enumerates each
position within the agency which involves the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on a financial interest and for each such
enumerated position, requires disclosure of specific types of
investments, business positions, interests in real property, and
sources of income. (Section 87302.)

The term "designated employee" means any officer, employee,
member or consultant of any agency whose position with the agency
is designated in a conflict of interest code because the position
entails the making or participation in the making of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial
interest. The term "designated employee" does not include any
unsalaried member of any board or commission which serves a solely
advisory function. (Section 82019.)

"Member" includes any salaried or unsalaried member of a
board or commission with decision-making authority. A board or
commission possesses decision-making authority whenever:

(A) It may make a final government decision;

(B) It may compel a governmental decision; or may prevent a
governmental decision either by reason of an exclusive
power to initiate the decision or by reason of a veto
which may not be overridden; or

(C) It makes substantive recommendations which are, and over
an extended period of time have been, regularly approved
without significant amendment or modification by another
public official or governmental agency.

Regulation 18700(a)(1).

Milne, Marie
MO. A-94-260
Page 3


It does not appear that members of GTFIPTP or its staff will
be making any final governmental decisions. GTFIPTP will make
recommendations to the Governor, but it does not appear that he is
bound by those recommendations. Therefore, it appears that
members of GTFIPTP and its staff serve a solely advisory
function.2/

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant




2     Under Regulation 18700(a)(1)(c), members of GTFIPTP may
become public officials subject to the Act's requirements if their
recommendations over an extended period of time are routinely
approved without significant amendment or modification by another
public official or government agency. It is our understanding
that GTFIPTP does not have a sunset date. If GTFIPTP continues to
exist for some time you should contact us for additional advice.
